patches.find: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF-14 / NOAA WMM2025 — derived from the INTERMAGNET observatory network) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author. Under the dome author's own definition ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled"), no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite — making even the weaker commit-timestamp test inapplicable: there is nothing to timestamp before the data arrives. The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets.
patches.replace: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. Westward magnetic drift has been a published observable since Edmund Halley's late-17th-century declination surveys — roughly 390 years of public record — and the modern rate is tabulated to high precision in NOAA's WMM2025 and ESA's IGRF-14 (derived from the INTERMAGNET observatory network and ESA Swarm L1B magnetometer data), all publicly indexed years before the V51 dome monitor commit. The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which a 390-year-old indexed observable plainly cannot satisfy — adopting it as a 2026 prediction is the disqualifier, not the absence of a timestamp. Mechanically, no monitor.py entry exists for this value — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite — so even the weaker commit-timestamp test that the dome author proposes ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled") has nothing to anchor; the monitoring absence is a symptom of the disqualifier, not the disqualifier itself.
patches.rationale: EXP-295 restructures the post-hoc paragraph to lead with the 390-year public-observable argument (the actual disqualifier per Part 3.1b), demoting monitor.py absence to a symptom. Closes ISS-1866 curmudgeon circularity complaint.

Critical wrong-author citation. CrossRef and OpenAlex both confirm doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122 is Loeb, Thorsen, Kato, Rose, Hodnebrog 2025 — not Raghuraman. Raghuraman et al. is a different 2021 PNAS paper (doi:10.1073/pnas.2113302118). Flagged by WIN-066.c3 hole #1 as critical.

patches.find: The dome's claimed angular drift rate of 0.0466 deg/yr also cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric (great-circle angular rate gives ~0.010–0.012 deg/yr; longitude regression gives ~0.038 deg/yr) — the dome's metric is undefined, rendering the claimed rate unverifiable.
patches.rationale: Verbatim duplication. The sentence appears identically at the end of paragraph 1 (its natural home) and again as the final sentence of the post-hoc paragraph. Remove the second trailing occurrence. Flagged by WIN-006.c3 hole #1 as major.

patches.find: The dome model specifies no numerical ratio, no time period, and no derivation — any observed value automatically "matches." CHAOS-7 describes the field; geodynamo theory explains it; the dome merely claims it.
patches.replace: The dome specifies a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry ratio but adopts it from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry — without a prior geometrically-derived prediction, any observed ratio automatically "matches." The dome additionally provides no time period and no physical mechanism connecting disc_radius or firmament_height to specifically 3:1. CHAOS-7 describes the field; geodynamo theory explains it; the dome merely relabels it.
patches.rationale: WIN-063.c3 hole (derived from the same finding as ISS-1894/1895): detail_verdict_text still asserts 'no numerical ratio' when the dome explicitly states 3:1. The fix preserves the core argument (no derivation → non-falsifiable) while being factually accurate about what the dome does and does not provide.
